summary: Iteration 43: Verified fix for ALL CAPS name parsing issue in ResumePreview.jsx. The parseContent() function now correctly handles ALL CAPS names like 'MARIA NIKITA' by distinguishing them from section headers using sectionKeywords pattern. All 10 unit tests pass. UI testing confirms name displays correctly in header across all templates.
passed_tests: ALL CAPS name parsing - 'MARIA NIKITA' displays correctly in header (not 'Your Name' placeholder)
Mixed case name parsing - 'Maria Nikita' displays correctly
Job title displayed below name in header
Contact information displayed correctly (email, phone, LinkedIn, location)
Editor width consistent at 794px across all modes (Preview, Edit Text, Edit Sections)
Template switching preserves name in header (tested Classic, Modern, Executive)
Section headers (PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY, WORK EXPERIENCE, etc.) correctly identified
Single word ALL CAPS names parsed correctly
Three word ALL CAPS names parsed correctly
critical_code_review_comments: Fix implemented correctly: sectionKeywords pattern (line 53) distinguishes section headers from names
isHeader() function (lines 55-65) now only treats ALL CAPS lines as headers if they contain section keywords
isLikelyName() function (lines 68-88) validates name-like patterns including ALL CAPS names
Code is clean and well-structured with proper comments
seed_data_creation: Test PDF files created at /tmp/test_resume_maria.pdf and /tmp/test_resume_mixed_case.pdf
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 43 verified the ALL CAPS name parsing fix. The parseContent() function in ResumePreview.jsx now correctly handles names in ALL CAPS by using sectionKeywords pattern to distinguish them from section headers. Unit tests added in ResumePreview.test.js cover all name parsing scenarios. The fix is working correctly in production.

rca_of_the_issue.bug_description: Resume header showed 'Your Name' placeholder instead of actual candidate name when name was in ALL CAPS
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The isHeader() function was treating any ALL CAPS line as a section header, including names like 'MARIA NIKITA'
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Added sectionKeywords pattern to distinguish section headers from names. Updated isHeader() to only treat ALL CAPS lines as headers if they contain section keywords. Added isLikelyName() function to validate name-like patterns.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 10 unit tests pass. UI testing confirms name displays correctly in header across all templates.
